title: Conjecture 1.1 (Gallai's Path Decomposition Conjecture)
statement_text: Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $\lceil \frac{n}{2} \rceil$ paths.
context_text: Posed by Gallai in answer to a question of Erdős (see [4]). It is sharp: if every vertex has odd degree, each vertex must be an endpoint of some path in any path decomposition, requiring at least $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ paths. Lovász proved that every graph on $n$ vertices has a decomposition into paths and cycles of size $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$, which settles the conjecture for graphs with at most one even-degree vertex.
paper_contribution: Proves Gallai's conjecture for all connected graphs $G$ with $\Delta(G) \leq 5$, by showing that any smallest counterexample cannot contain any of five reducible configurations, forcing $G_E$ to be a forest and applying Pyber's earlier theorem.
paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition conjecture for graphs of small maximum degree

title: Question 1.1
statement_text: Does every connected graph $G$ that is not an odd semi-clique admit a path decomposition into $\lfloor \frac{|V(G)|}{2} \rfloor$ paths?
context_text: An odd semi-clique is a graph obtained from a clique on $2k+1$ vertices by deleting at most $k-1$ edges; a simple counting argument shows such a graph cannot be decomposed into $k$ paths. The authors ask whether odd semi-cliques are the only obstructions to a stronger, ceiling-free version of Gallai's conjecture.
paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition conjecture for graphs of small maximum degree
